behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git rename detection plus CLI parsing/output. `MOCK-MISS`: this is not just local classification logic; it depends on how git reports renames for actual repo history. `REAL-INFRA`: requires real repository state/filesystem and typically real git subprocess output. `MOCK-HIDE`: fixture/mocked status output can miss rename-threshold and format edge cases. `LLM-DEP`/`MULTI-STEP`: not applicable. Rejected lower level: behavior text says the tool reads a git repository and classifies rename vs add+delete, which disqualifies pure Unit/Integration-only wiring tests as minimum for real failure capture.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, commit baseline file, rename it with content variations, run the CLI against real repo state, and assert report shows exactly one rename and zero paired add/delete for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handling real binary-diff behavior from git/OS I/O. `MOCK-MISS`: parser may pass on sanitized fixtures but fail on actual subprocess output/encoding edge cases. `REAL-INFRA`: real filesystem bytes + git subprocess behavior are central. `MOCK-HIDE`: mocked diff text can hide crash conditions from real binary blobs. `LLM-DEP`/`MULTI-STEP`: not applicable. Rejected Integration: behavior explicitly targets binary diffs from repository analysis, where real infra effects are the risk.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp git repo, add/modify at least one binary file (e.g., random bytes), run CLI on real repo changes, assert process exits successfully and binary files are included in category counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is no-commit repository semantics surfaced via real git command behavior/exit codes. `MOCK-MISS`: local logic tests may not reproduce true subprocess responses in unborn HEAD state. `REAL-INFRA`: needs actual repo initialized with zero commits. `MOCK-HIDE`: stubbing command results can mask incorrect handling of real git stderr/return codes. `LLM-DEP`/`MULTI-STEP`: not applicable. Rejected Integration: behavior is specifically about real repository state before first commit.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a fresh git repo with no commits, run CLI, assert exit is non-error and stdout is a valid empty structured report.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against real git history, crossing filesystem, git internals/subprocess output, and report generation. `MOCK-MISS`: module-level fakes can validate arithmetic but miss mismatches from real history extraction. `REAL-INFRA`: real repo commits/renames/deletes across history are required. `MOCK-HIDE`: mocked history payloads can accidentally mirror implementation assumptions. `LLM-DEP`/`MULTI-STEP`: not applicable. Rejected Integration: behavior text anchors correctness to actual repository history, not just module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo with a known sequence of commits including add/delete/rename/modify actions, run CLI, and assert reported category totals exactly match independently computed ground truth from git history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git index vs working-tree semantics, which are OS/repo-state dependent. `MOCK-MISS`: this specifically tests state handoff between two real git states, not a single pure function. `REAL-INFRA`: requires manipulating actual staged and unstaged changes in a real repo. `MOCK-HIDE`: stubs can miss subtle differences in git diff modes and path status emission. `LLM-DEP`/`MULTI-STEP`: not applicable. Rejected Integration: behavior text explicitly compares staged vs unstaged analysis modes, a real git-state boundary.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create equivalent change sets once staged and once unstaged, run CLI in both scenarios, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout.
